content: I'm burning out running my AI startup. I've been working 80-hour weeks since we launched last year, and my parents just moved in with me in Lisbon.
That sounds incredibly stressful. How old are your parents and what kind of care do they need?
Mom's 72 and has early-stage Parkinson's. Dad's 75 and mostly okay but can't drive anymore. I'm trying to manage everything from investor meetings to doctor appointments.
That's a heavy load. Have you considered hiring help or bringing on a co-founder to share the workload?
I've thought about it. We're pre-seed so cash is tight. I did hire a part-time developer last month though - my old college friend Sofia who's amazing with 3D printing and robotics.
That's great you have Sofia on board! How's the startup doing otherwise?
We just closed a small angel round - about €200,000. Enough to keep us going for 6-8 months. But I'm questioning if this is sustainable long-term.
What would you do if you weren't running this startup?
I'd probably go back to my woodworking hobby full-time. I've been making these intricate origami-inspired wooden lamps that people seem to love.
Those sound beautiful! Have you sold any?
Just through word of mouth so far. Made about €3,000 last quarter. But it's not enough to support everyone.
What does your partner think about all this?
I'm single, actually. Focused on the business and parents. My sister in Porto offered to take them but I couldn't do that to her - she has three kids already.
